page: Microsoft Azure D16d_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DDv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The configuration includes 16 virtual CPUs (8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, providing 2 threads per core) and 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. A 614 GB local storage disk is bundled with the instance, though it does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark evaluations place the D16d_v5 in the average performance tier across multiple tests. The processor achieved a stress-ng single-core score of 1898.68 and a multi-core score of 15521.76, complemented by a Geekbench single-core score of 1537.0 and a multi-core score of 9098.0. Memory performance tests show a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 832.64 GB/sec, which drops to 79.96 GB/sec for large uncached blocks, alongside a write bandwidth of 38.08 GB/sec. In database and application-level tests, the instance processed 15.33 million Redis SET operations per second and achieved a PassMark database operations score of 8008.48, alongside a static web serving throughput of 17.85 GB/sec.

The hardware configuration and benchmark results indicate that the D16d_v5 is suited for general-purpose workloads, web serving, database operations, and software compilation, as evidenced by its Geekbench clang compilation score of 14575.0. It also supports light machine learning tasks, delivering 35.07 tokens/sec for prompt processing on a 7B LLM model. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the inclusion of a 614 GB local disk alongside a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio provides a self-contained resource profile for applications requiring temporary local storage without the overhead of external storage attachments.
description: Microsoft Azure D16d_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DDv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is equipped with a dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C x86_64 processor operating at 2.8 GHz, delivering 16 vCPUs across 8 physical cores. The instance features 64.0 GB of system memory and a bundled 614 GB local storage disk, with no integrated GPU. Benchmark results place the server in the average tier, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 9098.0, a PassMark CPU score of 21133.57, and a Redis SET throughput of 15.33 million operations per second. The balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and local storage make this instance qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring local scratch space. It is well-suited for general-purpose applications, web serving, database operations, and software compilation.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D16d_v5 is a general-purpose VM featuring 16 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and a 614 GB local disk. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor, it is designed for databases, web serving, and general workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D16d_v5 is a general-purpose VM with 16 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and a 614 GB local disk, powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C CPU.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processing, 64 GB of memory, and integrated local storage for diverse workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 16 vCPUs and 8 physical cores
64.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Bundled 614 GB local storage disk for temporary data and scratch space
Average multi-core performance with 15.33 million Redis SET operations per second
Suitable for general-purpose computing, web serving, and database workloads
